message:
fix(ruby): Apply individual rule file scanning pattern

Applies the --json=stream individual file scanning pattern to modules/ubs-ruby.sh,
completing the systematic rollout across all language modules. This was
inadvertently omitted from commit 106c211.

Changes:
========

Same pattern as applied to JS/Python/Go/C++/Rust in commit 106c211:
- Scan each rule file individually in loop
- Use --json=stream instead of --json
- Initialize tmp_json with : >"$tmp_json" before loop
- Append results with >> in loop

Ruby has 1 async error rule:
- ruby.async.thread-no-rescue: Thread.new blocks without rescue

Impact:
=======

Ensures Ruby module uses same reliable scanning pattern as all other
modules. Once Ruby's run_async_error_checks() is fully tested, it will
benefit from the same reliability improvements as other languages.

This completes the async error detection infrastructure consistency work.
All 7 modules (JS, Python, Go, C++, Rust, Java, Ruby) now use identical
rule loading patterns.

Related:
- 106c211: Individual file scanning for 5 modules
- fd60a89: Rule directory pattern introduction

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -397,12 +397,16 @@ rule:
       kind: rescue_clause
 YAML
   tmp_json="$(mktemp 2>/dev/null || mktemp -t rb_async_matches.XXXXXX)"
-  if ! "${AST_GREP_CMD[@]}" scan -r "$rule_dir" "$PROJECT_DIR" --json >"$tmp_json" 2>/dev/null; then
-    rm -rf "$rule_dir"
-    rm -f "$tmp_json"
-    print_finding "info" 0 "ast-grep scan failed" "Unable to compute async error coverage"
-    return
-  fi
+  : >"$tmp_json"
+  local rule_file
+  for rule_file in "$rule_dir"/*.yml; do
+    if ! "${AST_GREP_CMD[@]}" scan -r "$rule_file" "$PROJECT_DIR" --json=stream >>"$tmp_json" 2>/dev/null; then
+      rm -rf "$rule_dir"
+      rm -f "$tmp_json"
+      print_finding "info" 0 "ast-grep scan failed" "Unable to compute async error coverage"
+      return
+    fi
+  done
   rm -rf "$rule_dir"
   if ! [[ -s "$tmp_json" ]]; then
     rm -f "$tmp_json"
